speed_delta.interpretation: Top5 is materially faster, saving 7.919 seconds on average, but its overall EDD score drops because quality and abstention reliability regress.

case_level_regressions.risk: Top5 reduced latency but introduced the largest quality loss; judge notes indicate weaker direct grounding and topic fit from narrower context.
Top5 was slightly slower and less relevant, so this query gains nothing from top_k=5.
Top5 speeds up a compare query but loses one groundedness point, suggesting compare-style prompts may need more evidence.
Top5 still retrieves the target but reduces answer completeness for an ambiguity-style query.
Top5 failed an expected abstention case. This is the adoption blocker despite the speed gain.

quality_improvement_exception.note: Top5 improved relevance on one precise-check case, so lower top_k can be safe for some tightly scoped questions.

top5_adoption_risk.recommendation: Do not adopt top_k=5 as the global default.
reasons: EDD score drops from 95.55 to 91.34 despite a 31.16 percent latency improvement.
Abstention accuracy drops from 1.0 to 0.5 due to qv2_021_abstain_winning_vendor.
Several non-abstention cases lose judge quality, including qv2_003 with a two-point combined groundedness/relevance loss.
Global retrieval metrics remain perfect, so the failures are likely context sufficiency or answer policy failures rather than simple retrieval misses.
top5_adoption_risk.safe_adoption_scope: Top5 is promising only as a selective fast path for low-risk precise_check and simple single_extract queries after gating; it should fall back to top8 for abstention, compare, ambiguous_org, followup, and any low-confidence retrieval pattern.

next_experiment_proposal.name: adaptive_top_k_fast_path_with_risk_fallback
next_experiment_proposal.hypothesis: An adaptive policy can preserve top8 quality and abstention behavior while capturing much of top5 latency savings by using top5 only for low-risk prompts and escalating to top8 for ambiguity, comparison, abstention-sensitive, or weak-context cases.

fallback_triggers: query asks for missing/nonexistent facts such as winner, vendor, contract amount, evaluation score, deadline, or undisclosed bid details
retrieved evidence spans multiple organizations/projects with no dominant project cluster
top5 answer would cite fewer than the required distinct project/document groups for compare or ambiguity prompts
generation contains uncertainty phrases about unavailable evidence while still producing a factual table/list; rerun with top8 and enforce abstention
fast_path_triggers: single dominant organization/project in retrieved set
requested answer can be satisfied from a small number of documents without cross-project comparison

experiment_arms.name: top8_champion_control
experiment_arms.purpose: Confirm baseline stability and keep champion comparison fixed.

experiment_arms.name: top5_global_speed_control
experiment_arms.purpose: Retain the known speed candidate as a negative quality control.

experiment_arms.name: adaptive_top5_else_top8_by_query_type
experiment_arms.policy: Use top5 only for precise_check and single_extract; use top8 for followup, compare, ambiguous_org, and abstention.
experiment_arms.purpose: Lowest-risk adaptive experiment; expected to recover most abstention and compare quality.

experiment_arms.name: adaptive_top5_with_abstention_guard
experiment_arms.policy: Use query-type policy plus force top8 and stricter abstention when the question requests procurement outcomes or facts likely absent from RFP documents.
experiment_arms.purpose: Directly target qv2_021 while preserving top5 wins on simple cases.

experiment_arms.name: adaptive_top6_middle_ground
experiment_arms.policy: Use top6 for fast-path queries and top8 for risk-triggered queries.
experiment_arms.purpose: Test whether one extra document repairs qv2_003-style grounding loss with less latency than top8.

required: abstention_accuracy >= 1.0 on the current eval set
edd_score >= 95.0 or within 0.5 of top8 champion
latency_avg_sec at least 15 percent faster than top8 champion
watch_items: qv2_003_single_add_large_transfer must recover at least groundedness 4 and relevance 5 or be routed to top8.
qv2_006_followup_incheon_committee_security should route to top8 because top5 was slower and lower quality.

next_experiment_proposal.expected_outcome: The query-type adaptive arm should keep top8 behavior for the riskiest 8 to 10 cases while using top5 on simpler cases. Based on observed per-case deltas, this should capture a meaningful but smaller latency gain than global top5 without accepting the abstention regression.

final_recommendation: Keep top8 as champion. Run the next experiment as adaptive_top5_else_top8_by_query_type first, then add the abstention guard and top6 middle-ground arm if the initial adaptive run still shows quality loss on qv2_003 or qv2_021.
notes: This worker output is proposal-only and does not edit final logs.
